questions.questionText: What is the correct name for an angle that is less than 90°?
questions.explanation: An acute angle is defined as an angle that measures less than 90°. An obtuse angle is between 90° and 180°. A right angle is exactly 90°.

questions.questionText: Two straight lines in a plane that never intersect are known as...
questions.explanation: Parallel lines are lines in a plane that are always the same distance apart and never meet.

questions.questionText: What does it mean if two lines are 'perpendicular'?
options: They are parallel to each other.
They intersect at an angle of 90°.
questions.explanation: Perpendicular lines are lines that intersect to form a right angle, which is exactly 90°.

questions.questionText: An angle that measures exactly 90° is called a...
questions.explanation: A right angle is an angle that measures exactly 90°. It is often marked with a small square symbol.

questions.questionText: Which term describes an angle that is greater than 90° but less than 180°?
questions.explanation: An obtuse angle is an angle with a measure that is greater than 90° and less than 180°.

questions.questionText: What is the name for a polygon that has five sides?
questions.explanation: A pentagon is a polygon with 5 sides. A hexagon has 6, a quadrilateral has 4, and an octagon has 8.

questions.questionText: What is the defining characteristic of a 'regular polygon'?
options: It has at least one right angle.
All its sides are different lengths.
All its sides and all its angles are equal.
It must have an even number of sides.
questions.explanation: A regular polygon is defined as a polygon where all sides have the same length and all interior angles have the same measure.

questions.questionText: An angle that measures exactly 180° is called a...
questions.explanation: A straight angle corresponds to a straight line and measures exactly 180°.

questions.questionText: What is the point where two lines meet to form an angle called?
questions.explanation: A vertex is the common endpoint of two lines or rays that form an angle.

questions.questionText: A triangle with two equal sides and two equal angles is called...
questions.explanation: An isosceles triangle is defined as a triangle that has two sides of equal length. This also results in two equal angles opposite those sides.

questions.questionText: How many diagonals does a quadrilateral have?
questions.explanation: A quadrilateral (a four-sided polygon) has two diagonals. A diagonal is a line segment connecting two non-adjacent vertices.

questions.questionText: An angle that is greater than 180° but less than 360° is known as a...
questions.explanation: A reflex angle is an angle that has a measure greater than 180° and less than 360°. It is the 'outside' angle.
